policy.title: RBI releases final guidelines on Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI mandates climate scenario analysis for all SCBs with assets above ₹10,000 crore by March 2027, requiring banks to integrate physical and transition risk into ICAAP. Guidelines specify minimum two climate scenarios (orderly and disorderly transition) with 30-year projection horizons.

policy.title: SEBI expands BRSR Core to top 500 listed companies, introduces limited assurance requirement
policy.description: SEBI extends mandatory BRSR Core reporting to top 500 companies by market cap from FY2026-27, with limited assurance required on nine key ESG indicators. Value chain Scope 3 emissions disclosure now mandatory for top 250 companies.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI issues draft framework for index-based parametric insurance products
policy.description: Draft guidelines permit insurers to offer parametric products tied to IMD weather indices, satellite data, and government-recognized climate triggers. Framework allows banks and NBFCs to act as corporate agents for distribution of parametric crop and property products.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: BCBS finalizes Pillar 3 climate risk disclosure framework with phased implementation
policy.description: Basel Committee publishes final standard requiring G-SIBs to disclose climate-related financial risks from January 2027, with D-SIBs following by 2028. Disclosures include concentration metrics for high-carbon sectors and physical risk exposures by geography.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk granularity
policy.description: Updated scenarios include sub-national physical risk projections for 45 emerging market jurisdictions including India, with district-level flood and heat stress data. New 'Current Policies Hot House' scenario models 3.2°C warming pathway impacts on bank credit portfolios.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: EFRAG publishes sector-specific ESRS for financial institutions under CSRD
policy.description: Financial sector standards require banks to report financed emissions intensity per sector and geography, plus physical and transition risk metrics aligned with EBA Pillar 3 templates. Standards apply to EU subsidiaries of non-EU banks from FY2027.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk analytics across loan portfolio
competitors.description: Partnership will integrate Jupiter's ClimateScore data into HDFC's corporate and MSME lending workflows, covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress perils. Initial phase covers 15,000 industrial and commercial properties across coastal India.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $12M Series A to expand climate risk modules for Indian banks
competitors.description: Round led by Lightspeed India with participation from existing investor Accel. Funds will support development of physical risk scoring for real estate collateral and expansion into Southeast Asian markets.

competitors.title: XDI launches India-specific physical risk API with pincode-level resolution
competitors.description: New product covers 19,000 pincodes with asset-level risk scores for flood, cyclone, extreme heat, and water stress. Pricing positioned at enterprise tier targeting Indian banks conducting RBI-mandated climate assessments.

competitors.title: Moody's integrates physical climate risk scores into India corporate credit ratings
competitors.description: Climate risk adjustment factors now applied to 450+ rated Indian corporates in power, metals, and real estate sectors. Methodology incorporates RCP 4.5 and 8.5 scenarios with explicit PD impact quantification.

competitors.title: CarbonMint partners with Federal Bank to tokenize carbon credits for agri-lending
competitors.description: Pilot program enables farmers with climate-smart agriculture practices to generate verified carbon credits as collateral enhancement for crop loans. Initial target of 50,000 farmers across Kerala and Karnataka.

competitors.title: Watershed raises $200M Series D, announces emerging markets expansion including India
competitors.description: Valuation reaches $2.8B as platform expands enterprise carbon accounting to cover Scope 3 in emerging market supply chains. India office opening planned for Q3 2026 with initial focus on export-oriented manufacturing.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Bank Portfolios: Methodology and Pilot Results
research.description: RBI pilot study across 12 PSU banks finds 8-14% of agricultural and MSME portfolios face high physical risk exposure, with coastal districts showing 2.3x higher expected credit losses under RCP 8.5 scenarios by 2040.

research.title: Parametric Insurance Solutions for Climate Resilience in South Asia
research.description: Report identifies $4.2B annual protection gap for climate perils in India, with parametric products potentially covering 60% of uninsured agricultural and SME exposures. Recommends regulatory sandbox approach and bank-insurer partnerships for distribution.

research.title: Sigma 1/2026: Natural Catastrophes and Climate Change in Emerging Markets
research.description: India experienced $12.8B in economic losses from climate events in 2025, with only 9% insured. Report projects annual losses to reach $35B by 2040, with flood and cyclone perils contributing 72% of total physical risk to financial assets.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps and Opportunities in Emerging Markets
research.description: IFC analysis of 2,400 emerging market companies finds only 23% have climate disclosures meeting ISSB standards, versus 67% in developed markets. India ranks highest among EM peers at 34% but significant gaps remain in Scope 3 and physical risk data.

research.title: Climate Scenario Analysis: Practices and Challenges for Emerging Market Banks
research.description: Survey of 85 EM banks reveals 62% lack internal capacity for climate scenario modeling, with data availability cited as primary constraint by 78% of respondents. Report recommends third-party hazard data integration and regulator-coordinated capacity building.

research.title: Innovative Risk Transfer Solutions for Climate Adaptation Finance
research.description: Analysis of 45 parametric insurance deployments globally finds basis risk reduced to under 8% with satellite-based triggers versus 15-20% for weather station indices. Recommends hybrid triggers combining multiple data sources for India's heterogeneous climate zones.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines create a $50M+ market for physical risk analytics that StepChange must capture before global players establish India beachheads.
body: This month marks an inflection point: Indian climate risk regulation has shifted from 'upcoming' to 'mandatory.' The convergence of RBI's final climate guidelines, SEBI's expanded BRSR requirements, and IRDAI's parametric insurance framework creates a synchronized demand signal across all three of StepChange's strategic bets. The window for establishing market position is now 12-18 months before compliance deadlines hit.

The RBI's final guidelines (policy-1) are the headline signal. The ₹10,000 crore threshold covers approximately 45 scheduled commercial banks, each now required to conduct climate scenario analysis with 30-year horizons. The RBI pilot study (research-1) revealing 8-14% high-risk exposure in agri and MSME portfolios provides exactly the kind of baseline data banks will need to benchmark against. StepChange should immediately develop a 'RBI Readiness Assessment' product that maps client portfolios against the pilot study methodology.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and XDI's India-specific API launch (comp-3) confirm that global climate risk players view India as a priority market. However, their enterprise-tier pricing and limited local presence create an opening. Sprih's $12M raise (comp-2) signals that Indian competitors are also mobilizing. StepChange's advantage lies in combining hazard data with PD/LGD translation that resonates with Indian credit risk teams—neither Jupiter nor XDI offer this natively. The next 90 days should focus on securing 3-4 pilot banks before Jupiter's HDFC work produces referenceable case studies.

The World Bank parametric insurance report (research-2) quantifying a $4.2B protection gap is a gift for StepChange's second strategic bet. Combined with IRDAI's draft framework permitting bank distribution of parametric products (policy-3), there's a clear path to help banks design climate-linked products rather than just assess risk. The Munich Re finding on satellite-based triggers reducing basis risk to 8% (research-6) provides technical validation for the hybrid trigger approach StepChange should recommend to bank partners.

Watch: EFRAG's financial sector ESRS (policy-6) will eventually reach Indian banks with EU operations—track which domestic banks have EU subsidiaries. Action: Schedule meetings with top 10 PSU banks in April referencing RBI's pilot study results; offer to benchmark their portfolios against the 8-14% high-risk finding as a door-opener.
